title: SecureYeoman Community Skill
description: Schema for a community-contributed SecureYeoman skill

description.description: Short description shown in the marketplace (max 2000 chars)

author.description: Skill author. May be a plain string (legacy) or a structured object with attribution links.

oneOf.description: Legacy string form — your GitHub username or real name.
Structured author object with optional attribution links.

license.description: SPDX license identifier (e.g. 'MIT', 'Apache-2.0'). Defaults to project license if omitted.

category.description: Skill category — must match the directory name

instructions.description: Full agent instructions. Minimum 100 words recommended for quality bar.

triggerPatterns.description: Regex patterns (case-insensitive) that gate injection of this skill's instructions into the system prompt. When the user message matches any pattern, the full instructions block is injected. Falls back to skill-name keyword matching when empty. Use specific phrases: e.g. 'review.*code|code.*review', '\bpr\b|pull.?request'.

useWhen.description: Conditions under which this skill should activate. Injected into the skill catalog in the system prompt so the model understands activation intent. Plain language — e.g. 'user asks to review a PR or diff'.

doNotUseWhen.description: Conditions under which this skill should NOT activate. Prevents false positives. Plain language — e.g. 'writing new code from scratch or debugging a runtime error'.

successCriteria.description: How the model knows the skill is complete. Appended after skill instructions. State concrete output requirements — e.g. 'Review complete with: summary, critical issues, suggestions, and at least one positive'.

mcpToolsAllowed.description: When non-empty, only these MCP tool names are available while this skill is active. Empty (default) means all enabled tools are available. Use to scope security-sensitive skills.

routing.description: Routing mode. 'fuzzy' (default) — model uses judgment. 'explicit' — appends deterministic routing text: 'To perform [Skill Name] tasks, use the [Skill Name] skill.' Use explicit for SOPs and compliance workflows.
